hero.headline: I design interfaces for the AI era
hero.tagline: Designing for AI means absorbing friction, latency and the unpredictable — and turning them into reliable experiences.

presentationHero.headline: I design interfaces for AI.
presentationHero.tagline: The orchestrator is the new designer. I design the workflows that make AI systems reason, from agent mapping to the interface that drives them.

pitchPlayground.title: Designing for AI is a different craft.
pitchPlayground.body: AI interfaces don't respond — they reason, hesitate, err and self-correct. Designing for them means architecting uncertainty as much as screens.

playground.title: What AI design challenge concerns you?
playground.subtitle: Pick a challenge, adjust creativity.
playground.ctaPostInteraction: Want to design a similar agent UI? Let's talk

about.title: 19 years of design, an AI speciality
about.bio: After 19 years designing interfaces for complex products, I pivoted to AI Design in 2024 — not out of trend-chasing, but because agents fundamentally change the UX problem. I design systems that absorb model uncertainty and make it invisible to the user. From Design Systems to agent workflows, I work where design rigour meets LLM logic.

contact.title: An AI project in mind?
contact.subtitle: Freelance · Full-time · Consulting — AI Design focused

meta.title: Julie Tyrode — Background & AI Design Expertise
meta.description: 19 years of Product Design, a deliberate pivot to AI in 2024. Discover Julie Tyrode's journey as AI Orchestrator & UX Designer.

hero.headline: 19 years of design. A pivot in 2024.
hero.tagline: I didn't discover AI in 2023 like everyone else. I waited until I understood what it actually changed about the UX problem.

pivot.title: Why AI Design is a different craft.
pivot.body1: For 19 years, I designed deterministic interfaces. We defined a state, a component, an interaction — and the system behaved exactly as specified.
pivot.body2: In 2024, I started working with language models on real client projects. And I understood that the design problem had changed in nature: the interface must now absorb the uncertainty, latency, and errors of a system that reasons.
pivot.body3: It's no longer a problem of state or flow — it's a problem of trust. How do you make an agent intelligible? How do you design waiting when the duration is unknown? How do you signal a hallucination without alarming the user?
pivot.body4: That's what I build now: interfaces that make AI work for humans.
pivot.quote: The orchestrator is the new designer.
